feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Martha Lillard, last US polio patient using iron lung, dies at 78 in Oklahoma
entry.summary: Martha Lillard, the last U.S. polio patient using an iron lung, has died at 78 in Oklahoma. Her sister, Cindy McVey, attributes her death to long-haul COVID-19. Diagnosed with polio at 5, Lillard lived a remarkable life despite her challenges. She attended sc…

entry.title: Box Elder County declares emergency amid 'severe' milk production loss
entry.summary: Box Elder County officials have declared a local state of emergency after a sample from a dairy farm in the county tested positive for highly pathogenic avian influenza.

entry.title: Your resting heart rate could reveal more about your health than you think, doctors say
entry.summary: Here is what your resting heart rate reveals about cardiovascular health and when to see your doctor. Pulse checks and wearable devices can help with measuring the rate.

entry.title: Study shows how toxic RFK Jr.’s change to measles vaccine is for US toddlers
entry.summary: The children who get a combination shot are some of the most vulnerable.

entry.title: Urgent warning as brain-swelling disease surges in multiple Southern California neighborhoods
entry.summary: A dangerous mosquito-borne virus that can cause deadly brain swelling is surging across Orange County — and officials are now sending trucks into one hard-hit city to fight back.

entry.title: Bryan Johnson Says an Incurable Autoimmune Disease Has Changed His Longevity Mission
entry.summary: Bryan Johnson has built a public profile around trying to slow aging, measure his body in extreme detail, and push longevity science beyond normal wellness....

entry.title: Fatal Rattlesnake Bite in Orange County Sparks Safety Concerns When Hiking
entry.summary: A rare and tragic incident on a Quail Hill trail earlier this year highlights seasonal rattlesnake activity and the importance of outdoor safety precautions.

entry.title: Dozens hospitalized with cyclosporiasis as cases of gastrointestinal illness spike in 31 states
entry.summary: Nearly 3,000 people have been sickened with a gastrointestinal illness called cyclosporiasis, a foodborne infection marked by intense, watery diarrhea, state health officials report.

entry.title: Lina Hidalgo sidelined by unexpected 'kissing disease' diagnosis
entry.summary: Harris County Judge Lina Hidalgo says she'll reduce public appearances for several weeks after being diagnosed with infectious mononucleosis, also known as mono

entry.title: Common Virus Triggers Parkinson's-Like Brain Damage in Mice
entry.summary: There's a long-standing hypothesis around Parkinson's disease: that one of the contributors could be viruses that knock out key neurons in the brain, which then makes someone more vulnerable to other genetic and environmental risk factors.

entry.title: They met while being treated for brain injuries. Now they’re getting married.
entry.summary: The couple reconnected years after meeting in a Minnesota hospital, and bonded over their experiences: “I’ve dealt with that, sweetie. I understand that.”

entry.title: The Best Morning Drink for Better Liver Health, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: Your beverage choices could be impacting your liver. Dietitians explain how this morning drink may support long-term liver health.

entry.title: Coffee may have powerful effect on liver health, major study suggests
entry.summary: Heavy coffee drinkers showed a 47% lower risk of hepatocellular carcinoma and reduced liver fat, liver iron and fibroinflammation in a major study.

entry.title: The data that led an AI biotech to pick up Novo's Parkinson's stem cell therapy
entry.summary: Cellular Intelligence's STEM-PD data, presented at ISSCR and in Nature Medicine, shows safety signals and reduced medication use in Parkinson's patients ahead of a planned Phase 2.

entry.title: Scientists Expected One Result From Alcohol and Alzheimer’s—They Found Another
entry.summary: Scientists found alcohol has opposite effects on the Alzheimer's brain—and the results left researchers stunned.

entry.title: This syndrome linked to heavy cannabis use is often misdiagnosed
entry.summary: Cannabinoid hyperemesis syndrome causes abdominal pain, nausea and vomiting that is serious enough to send people to the emergency room.

entry.title: Common daily food may slow biological aging in men: study
entry.summary: A simple lifestyle adjustment could potentially slow down the body’s biological aging process, according to new research.

entry.title: An intestinal parasite is causing ‘explosive’ diarrhea in Pa. and N.J. Here’s what to know about cyclospora
entry.summary: If left untreated, symptoms can last anywhere between a few days and a month or longer.

entry.title: Officials to release list of buildings linked to Legionnaires' outbreak on Upper East Side
entry.summary: As of Friday morning, 46 people have tested positive for the illness, and 22 remain hospitalized.

entry.title: Lawmakers Call for CDC to Track Vitamin K Shot Refusals, Cite ProPublica Report
entry.summary: Two members of Congress are urging federal officials to monitor vitamin K shot refusals by parents and the potential consequences for their newborns, including severe bleeding or death.

entry.title: CDC Investigates Parasite Outbreak Causing ‘Explosive Diarrhea’ as More Than 1,200 People Are Sickened
entry.summary: The CDC is investigating a multistate cyclosporiasis outbreak linked to contaminated food or water, with nearly 1,000 cases reported in Michigan alone. Here’s what we know.

entry.title: Ebola death toll reaches 600, as new cases suspected in other parts of Congo
entry.summary: New suspected cases of Ebola have been reported in parts of Congo that were previously unaffected, the government said, signaling the continued spread of the disease beyond the epicenter in Ituri.

entry.title: Millions who rely on US-funded HIV/AIDS programs face uncertainty as financial cliff looms
entry.summary: Millions of HIV/AIDS patients, many of them in Africa, face an uncertain future as a financial cliff-edge for US-funded global programs fast approaches.

entry.title: Pioneering treatment saves identical twins from rare pregnancy condition
entry.summary: Identical twins Nancy and Margo benefitted from the procedure while in the womb as part of a world-first medical trial.

entry.title: What to know about 'explosive diarrhoea' parasite outbreak in US
entry.summary: The source of the cyclosporiasis outbreak, which has spread across at least 17 states, is unclear.

entry.title: Julie DeVuono, former nurse practitioner, gets historic $544,000 state penalty for falsifying pediatric vaccine records
entry.summary: This is the largest civil penalty imposed for vaccination fraud in the Department of Health's 125-year history, officials said.

entry.title: Long Island nurse who made $1.5M selling fake COVID vaccine cards slapped with $544K fine
entry.summary: “This is the largest civil penalty imposed for vaccination fraud in the Department of Health’s 125-year history,” State Health Commissioner Dr. James McDonald said of the massive $544,000 fin…

entry.title: Fast Walkers Could Cut Their Risk of Cognitive Decline in Half, New Study Finds
entry.summary: Fast walking may lower dementia and cognitive decline risk by 50%, new research finds. Learn how walking habits impact long-term brain health.

entry.title: Men’s Testosterone Levels Have Fallen by Half in 50 Years and Scientists Are Worried
entry.summary: "We saw on over 50% decline," researcher says.

entry.title: Parasitic stomach illness that can cause explosive diarrhea rises in Louisiana
entry.summary: Louisiana health officials are tracking 23 cases of cyclosporiasis, a foodborne parasitic illness that can cause weeks of watery diarrhea.

entry.title: C1 Neurons Identified as Master Anxiety Switch
entry.summary: A new study identifies brainstem C1 neurons as precise modulators of chronic trauma and long-term anxiety.

entry.title: Pennsylvania measles cases surpass 100, now spreading in 7 counties
entry.summary: False

entry.title: This US region sees its first locally acquired 'kissing bug' disease case
entry.summary: The first locally acquired Chagas case, a parasitic illness known as “kissing bug” disease, has been confirmed in this region.

entry.title: How to handle the heatwave mosquito surge
entry.summary: Scientists say this summer has seen the perfect conditions for the insects to thrive. Here’s how the experts stop getting bitten alive

entry.title: In Congo, health workers fighting Ebola go on strike after months without pay
entry.summary: Doctors and Nurses on the front lines of the Democratic Republic of Congo’s Ebola response have reached a breaking point.

entry.title: Nearly 300 cases of cyclosporiasis reported in NYC, additional 121 across state, health officials say
entry.summary: Nearly 300 cases of cyclosporiasis​ have been reported in New York City alone over the past two months, health officials said Wednesday.

entry.title: Learning What Substance Is Suspected of Causing Alzheimer's May Throw You Into an Existential Crisis
entry.summary: A surprisingly common substance was found to have a causal relationship with the kind of brain damage associated with Alzheimer's disease.

entry.title: Brits' Plan in 1788 to Stop Smallpox May Have Backfired
entry.summary: Ships arriving in Australia may have had scabs from survivors intended as a preventative

entry.title: Does This 400-Year-Old Renaissance Painting Show An Animal Behavior Only ‘Discovered’ In 2025?
entry.summary: Studying old paintings can give us a surprising glimpse of historic natural history.

entry.title: WHO: Global Cancer Cases May Surge Almost 70% by 2050
entry.summary: But around 4 in 10 cases are tied to known risk factors like smoking

entry.title: Cluster of extremely rare cancer cases suddenly strikes kids in wealthy SoCal city
entry.summary: The families say they have no proof of what may be behind the diagnoses but are urging officials to examine whether pesticides used throughout the community could be a factor.

entry.title: Cyclospora, a stomach-churning parasite, has reached Mass. Here’s what to know.
entry.summary: Patients are experiencing chronic diarrhea as health officials scramble to find the source. Massachusetts has become one of more than a dozen states to report cases since May 1.

entry.title: Major breakthrough for cancer treatment
entry.summary: Plus: CTA crime drops, Taste of Chicago must-eats and more.

entry.title: Ozempic Face, Blindness Claims, And Billions In Potential Damages: The GLP-1 Reckoning
entry.summary: Over 4,400 lawsuits target Ozempic and GLP-1 drugs for GI injuries, vision loss, and "Ozempic face." Usage stats, shortages, and updates.

entry.title: Strength Training These 3 Muscles Will Lead to the Biggest Heart Health Benefits, Doctor Says
entry.summary: Strength training bigger muscles can give your heart a workout, says Dr. Joseph Herrera, a sports medicine expert. Learn more about which muscle groups are the best to train.

entry.title: Genomic epidemiology of the ongoing 2026 Bundibugyo Virus Disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo
entry.summary: Genomic epidemiology of the ongoing 2026 Bundibugyo Virus Disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo Institut National de Recherche Biomédicale (INRB), Kinshasa – DRC and partners Context On 15 May 2026, the Ministry of Public Health, Hygiene a…

entry.title: Column | What a sleep expert eats in a day for better rest at night
entry.summary: This professor’s research has found that certain foods are associated with deep, more restorative sleep.

entry.title: 90 percent of U.S. adults have this syndrome — but most have never heard of it
entry.summary: New guidelines may soon make the disorder known as CKM syndrome a common diagnosis.

entry.title: Is This the End of Booze?
entry.summary: The turn against moderate drinking has been sudden and sharp. But it's not often based in science.

entry.title: An Illegal Injectable Tanning Drug Is Trending on TikTok – And It's Giving People Dangerous Moles
entry.summary: Dermatologists in multiple countries are expressing concern about TikTok influencers promoting what is known by some as 'the Barbie drug'.
